speaker: patient
text: Hi, Doctor. I’ve been feeling more tired than usual lately, and my ankles have been swelling up quite a bit.

speaker: doctor
text: Thanks for coming in. Let me check your vitals — your heart rate today is 93 bpm.

speaker: doctor
text: Your blood pressure is elevated at 156 over 92 mmHg. That’s consistent with what we’ve seen before.

speaker: patient
text: Yeah, it’s been running high for a few weeks now. I’ve been taking my medication regularly, but I’m not sure it’s working as well.

speaker: doctor
text: You’re currently on Amlodipine 10mg once daily — that’s what we started last month.

speaker: doctor
text: Based on your persistent elevation and symptoms, the diagnosis remains Essential Hypertension.

speaker: doctor
text: Your hemoglobin came back at 85 g/L — that’s significantly low and could explain your fatigue.

speaker: patient
text: Oh, that’s much lower than my last blood test — I remember it was around 115 g/L two months ago.

speaker: patient
text: And yes, the swelling in my lower limbs has gotten worse — especially by evening.

speaker: doctor
text: That edema, combined with the low hemoglobin and uncontrolled BP, raises concern for possible heart strain or underlying anemia-related cardiac compensation.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve also noticed shortness of breath when climbing stairs — didn’t used to happen.

speaker: doctor
text: Let’s order a full CBC, ferritin, B12, folate, and an echocardiogram to assess cardiac function and rule out secondary causes.

speaker: patient
text: Okay — and about the swelling: could the Amlodipine be making it worse? I read it can cause edema.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s a very good point — peripheral edema is a known side effect of calcium channel blockers like Amlodipine.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your worsening edema and the new finding of severe anemia, I am going to switch you from Amlodipine to Lisinopril 10mg daily, which is less likely to cause fluid retention.

speaker: patient
text: Lisinopril — okay. Should I stop the Amlodipine cold turkey?

speaker: doctor
text: Yes, discontinue Amlodipine today and start Lisinopril tomorrow morning. Monitor for dizziness or cough, and call if you develop swelling of the lips or tongue.

speaker: patient
text: Got it. And about the low hemoglobin — do you think it’s from iron deficiency? I’ve had some dark stools recently.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s important — occult GI bleeding is a key concern with iron-deficiency anemia. We’ll add fecal occult blood testing and refer you for colonoscopy next week.

speaker: patient
text: Thank you, Doctor. I’ll follow up with the labs and call if the ankle swelling doesn’t improve in a few days.
